# benches

Criterion benchmark suite for `attractor_dot::parse`. Measures parser throughput on synthetic DOT graphs ranging from simple chains to large subgraph hierarchies. Generates deterministic test fixtures programmatically to avoid I/O overhead during measurement.

## Contents

- **[parse.rs](./parse.rs)** — Defines `bench_parse_simple`, `bench_parse_with_attributes`, `bench_parse_large_graphs`, `bench_parse_subgraphs`, `bench_parse_chained_edges`. Registers suite via `criterion_group!` and executes via `criterion_main!`. Contains `generate_large_graph` and `generate_subgraph_graph` test data builders.

## Behavioral Contracts

Test data generation uses format templates:
- Node declaration: `node{} [shape="box",label="Node {}",max_retries="3",timeout="30s"]`
- Edge declaration: `node{} -> node{};`
- Subgraph cluster: `subgraph cluster_{} { inner{}_a -> inner{}_b; }`

Benchmark `bench_parse_with_attributes` validates parsing of: `shape="Mdiamond"`, `max_retries=3`, `timeout=30s`, `goal_gate=true`, `prompt="Do work"`, `fidelity="full"`.

Graph sizing parameters:
- `generate_large_graph` pre-allocates `node_count * 80 + 50` bytes capacity
- `bench_parse_large_graphs` sweeps node counts `[10, 50, 100, 200]`
- `bench_parse_subgraphs` sweeps cluster counts `[5, 10, 20]`
- `bench_parse_chained_edges` parses 8-node chain: `A -> B -> C -> D -> E -> F -> G -> H [label="chain", weight=10]`

## File Relationships

Benchmarks target `attractor_dot::parse` interface (implementation resides in `../src/parser.rs`). No external file dependencies; all fixtures generated in-memory via `generate_large_graph` and `generate_subgraph_graph`.
